frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: evidence integration with arousal-gated thresholds and decision boundaries

frameworks.name: neuromodulatory gain control
frameworks.one_line: locus coeruleus and basal forebrain projections set global decision state

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: perceptual choice bias decomposed from sensitivity

core_question: Does ascending brainstem arousal dynamically modulate perceptual decision biases trial-to-trial rather than this variability being mere noise?
key_assumptions: pupil diameter indexes global brainstem arousal state
tonic and phasic pupil components reflect dissociable neuromodulatory signals
evidence integration can be captured by a drift-diffusion process
manipulations: repeated presentations of the same perceptual evidence
measurement of tonic vs phasic pupil epochs around evidence onset
key_findings: tonic pupil predicts trial-to-trial direction and magnitude of decision bias
phasic pupil after evidence onset predicts subsequent choice variability
choice variability shifts systematically across conditions consistent with pupil-bias coupling
interpretations: part of decision variability reflects state-dependent brainstem arousal modulation, not noise
arousal dynamically gates evidence integration thresholds and decision boundaries
